title: cordia guineensis
intro: Cordia guineensis, also known as the West African Cordia, is a plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Cordia guineensis include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve respiratory health. Cordia guineensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine in West Africa to make ointments and salves for topical application.
